messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following event ticketing platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"TicketHub collects your name, email, and payment details to process ticket purchases. We also collect event attendance history to recommend future events. Payment processing is handled by PayPal (EU). Ticket data is retained for 3 years. With your consent, we send you event recommendations. You can manage your preferences or delete your account in the app."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) implied for ticket purchases. Consent implied for marketing. Separate lawful basis for event history-based recommendations not stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Event attendance can reveal religious beliefs (e.g., faith events), political affiliation, or health interests. No safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Ticket processing and event recommendations are stated. Whether attendance history is used for profiling or third-party data sharing is not addressed.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate for ticket purchasing and event recommendation.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] 3-year retention for ticket data stated. Retention for email addresses used for marketing is not addressed.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Account deletion (erasure) and preference management (partial objection/restriction) referenced via the app. Formal rights not listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] PayPal EU named as payment processor.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] PayPal EU is stated, but PayPal's global infrastructure may involve non-EEA transfers — not addressed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (recommendations lawful basis not stated), Art. 9 (event attendance inference risk not addressed), Art. 5 (marketing email retention absent), Art. 15-21 (formal rights not listed), Art. 46 (PayPal transfer safeguards not referenced), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State separate lawful basis for event history-based recommendations
- Acknowledge that event attendance may reveal religious or political beliefs and state safeguards
- Add marketing email retention period
- Add a formal data subject rights section
- Reference PayPal transfer safeguards
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
